LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT LAWRENCE EBRINGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT STATES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM FOR THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED THAT DEFENDANT KNEW OF THE DEFECTS IN THE HOME AND DID NOT DISCLOSE THEM. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON FRAUD. PLAINTIFF POINTS TO SPECIFIC MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE BY DEFENDANT TO INDUCE PLAINTIFF TO PURCHASE THE PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. The objection that the term "prepared," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide verified, amended responses to these RFAs according to the definition clarified in the March 20, 2014 letter from Plaintiffs' counsel (defined as to look at letters or words or to become acquainted with or look over the contents of a writing) RFAs 24-40: The objection that the term "reviewed," is vague and ambiguous and unintelligible is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide verified, amended responses to these RFAs according to the definition clarified in the March 20, 2014 letter from Plaintiffs' counsel.The term prepared to blank portions of a standard form, contract or disclosure. RFAs 3, 10-13,15, 17-23: The objection that the term "received," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide verified, amended responses to these RFAs according to the definition clarified in the March 20, 2014 letter from Plaintiffs' counsel. FI 9.1: The objection that the term "incident," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs' attorney client privilege and attorney work product objections are SUSTAINED. Plaintiffs shall provide complete and straightforward responses to subparts (a) - (d).FI 17.1: The objection that the term "prepared," is vague and ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs shall provide complete and straightforward responses to subparts (a) - (d) as to RFAs 1-2, 4-9, 14, and 16. RFP 15: Plaintiffs' attorney-client privilege and attorney work product objections are SUSTAINED. Plaintiffs' objection as to "any other applicable privilege'" is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs' objection that the "interrogatory (sic) seeks expert witness information is SUSTAINED. Plaintiffs objection that the request "seeks information of third parties'" is OVERRULED. The objection that the terms "estimate," "work," and "must be performed" are ambiguous is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs' relevancy objection is OVERRULED. Sanctions in the amount of $1,000 are awarded against Plaintiffs Stephen West and Aimee West. Verified, amended responses to be provided by Plaintiff within 10 days of service of this order.
